8.0 Data Quality Assessment

# Step 2: follow up on suspicious mins/maxes flagged above
print("tempo == 0:",       (df['tempo'] == 0).sum())
print("duration_ms == 0:", (df['duration_ms'] == 0).sum())
print("energy == 0:",      (df['energy'] == 0).sum())
print("loudness > 0:",     (df['loudness'] > 0).sum())

# Step 3: object columns
obj_cols = ['track_id','artists','album_name','track_name','track_genre']
for col in obj_cols:
    print(f"{col} — Unique: {df[col].nunique()}, Blank: {(df[col].str.strip()=='').sum()}")

# Step 4: other checks
print(df['track_id'].str.len().value_counts())
print(df['time_signature'].value_counts().sort_index())
print(df['explicit'].value_counts())

8.4 Validity
In [544]:
# Out-of-range check for bounded features
range_cols = ['danceability','energy','speechiness','acousticness',
              'instrumentalness','liveness','valence']
for col in range_cols:
    print(f"{col} out of [0,1]:", ((df[col] < 0) | (df[col] > 1)).sum())

# Other range checks
print("popularity out of [0,100]:", ((df['popularity'] < 0) | (df['popularity'] > 100)).sum())
print("key out of [0,11]:",         ((df['key'] < 0) | (df['key'] > 11)).sum())
print("mode out of [0,1]:",         ((df['mode'] < 0) | (df['mode'] > 1)).sum())

# Suspicious values (invalid zero values)
print("tempo > 250 BPM:",      (df['tempo'] > 250).sum())


# Check Duration outliers
df['duration_min'] = df['duration_ms'] / 60000
print("duration > 15 min:",    (df['duration_min'] > 15).sum())
print("duration > 30 min:",    (df['duration_min'] > 30).sum())


# Check zero popualrity tracks

print((df["popularity"] == 0).sum())
danceability out of [0,1]: 0 energy out of [0,1]: 0 speechiness out of [0,1]: 0 acousticness out of [0,1]: 0 instrumentalness out of [0,1]: 0 liveness out of [0,1]: 0 valence out of [0,1]: 0 popularity out of [0,100]: 0 key out of [0,11]: 0 mode out of [0,1]: 0 tempo > 250 BPM: 0 duration > 15 min: 153 duration > 30 min: 36 16020

track_id 0 artists 0 album_name 0 track_name 0 popularity 0 duration_ms 0 explicit 0 danceability 0 energy 0 key 0 loudness 0 mode 0 speechiness 0 acousticness 0 instrumentalness 0 liveness 0 valence 0 tempo 0 time_signature 0 track_genre 0 duration_min 0 dtype: int64

df = df[df["duration_ms"] > 0]
In [548]:
# Step 4: Impute zero tempo with median

median_tempo = df[df["tempo"] > 0]["tempo"]. median()

df["tempo"] = df["tempo"].replace(0,median_tempo) # replace zeros with median
print(median_tempo)
122.028
In [549]:
# Step 5: Engineering feature variables

# Transform explicit boolean to binary for modelling
df["explicit_int"] = df["explicit"].astype(int)

# Assign tier to popularity variable for descriptive analysis
df["popularity_tier"] = pd.cut(df['popularity'],
  bins = [0, 20, 40, 60, 80, 100],
  labels = ["Very Low", "Low", "Medium", "High", "Very High"])
In [550]:
# Step 6: Standardise text formats

# Strip leading/trailing whitespace from all text columns
for col in ['artists', 'album_name', 'track_name', 'track_genre']:
    df[col] = df[col].str.strip()

# Standardise track_genre to lowercase for consistent matching
df['track_genre'] = df['track_genre'].str.lower()

# Verify no double spaces remain
print("Text standardisation:")
for col in ['artists', 'album_name', 'track_name']:
    double_space = df[col].str.contains(r'  ', na=False).sum()
    print(f"  {col} double spaces: {double_space}")
Text standardisation: artists double spaces: 0 album_name double spaces: 0 track_name double spaces: 0
In [551]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Step 7: Check outliers in the numerical columns

num_cols = ['popularity','danceability','energy','loudness','speechiness',
            'acousticness','instrumentalness','liveness','valence','tempo','duration_min']

outlier_counts = []

for col in num_cols:
    Q1 = df[col].quantile(0.25)
    Q3 = df[col].quantile(0.75)
    IQR = Q3 - Q1
    lower = Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
    upper = Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
    outliers = ((df[col] < lower) | (df[col] > upper)).sum()
    outlier_counts.append(outliers)
    print(f"{col} — outliers: {outliers}")  # ← indented inside loop

# Bar chart
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.barh(num_cols, outlier_counts)
plt.xlabel('Number of Outliers')
plt.title('IQR Outlier Count per Feature')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('outlier_barchart.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()
popularity — outliers: 2 danceability — outliers: 615 energy — outliers: 0 loudness — outliers: 6115 speechiness — outliers: 13167 acousticness — outliers: 0 instrumentalness — outliers: 25131 liveness — outliers: 8610 valence — outliers: 0 tempo — outliers: 479 duration_min — outliers: 5592
In [552]:
# Step 8: Normalisation (min-max scaling)

# Apply mix-max scaling to the numeric variables

scaling_cols = ['danceability', 'energy', 'loudness', 'speechiness', 'acousticness',
              'instrumentalness', 'liveness', 'valence', 'tempo', 'duration_min']

for col in scaling_cols:
    col_min = df[col].min()
    col_max = df[col].max()
    df[col + '_norm'] = ((df[col] - col_min) / (col_max - col_min)).round(4) # This rescale values to [0,1] so that no feature dominates the model.

norm_cols = [c + '_norm' for c in scaling_cols]
print(df[norm_cols].agg(['min', 'max']).round(4))

# Sample view of comparision of before and after normalisation (loudness varaible taken as example)

print(df[['track_name', 'loudness', 'loudness_norm']].head(10).to_string())
danceability_norm energy_norm loudness_norm speechiness_norm \ min 0.0 0.0 0.0 0.0 max 1.0 1.0 1.0 1.0 acousticness_norm instrumentalness_norm liveness_norm valence_norm \ min 0.0 0.0 0.0 0.0 max 1.0 1.0 1.0 1.0 tempo_norm duration_min_norm min 0.0 0.0 max 1.0 1.0 track_name loudness loudness_norm 0 Comedy -6.746 0.7914 1 Ghost - Acoustic -17.235 0.5974 2 To Begin Again -9.734 0.7361 3 Can't Help Falling In Love -18.515 0.5737 4 Hold On -9.681 0.7371 5 Days I Will Remember -8.807 0.7533 6 Say Something -8.822 0.7530 7 I'm Yours -9.331 0.7436 8 Lucky -8.700 0.7552 9 Hunger -6.770 0.7909

print("Final shape:       ", df.shape)
print("Missing values:    ", df.isnull().sum().sum())
print("Duplicate rows:    ", df.duplicated().sum())
print("Zero tempo:        ", (df['tempo'] == 0).sum())
print("Zero duration_ms:  ", (df['duration_ms'] == 0).sum())
print("Original columns:  ", 20)
print("New columns added: ", df.shape[1] - 20)

In [554]:

df.to_csv("dataset_clean_final.csv", index=False)

Cleaned dataset saved as dataset_clean_final.csv
 

10.0 Exploratory Data Analysis




 
10.1 Univariate Analysis

 
10.1.1 Popularity Distribution
In [555]:
# Target Variable - Popularity

#Populrity Skewness and kurtosis

print("Skewness:", round(df['popularity'].skew(), 3))
print("Kurtosis:", round(df['popularity'].kurtosis(),3))
print("Mean:", round(df['popularity'].mean(),3))
print("Median:", round(df['popularity'].median(),3))


# Histogram of popularity scores

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.hist(df['popularity'], bins=50)
plt.axvline(df['popularity'].mean(), color='red', linestyle='--', label='Mean')
plt.axvline(df['popularity'].median(), color='gold', linestyle='--', label='Median')
plt.title("Distribution of Popularity Scores")
plt.xlabel("Popularity Score")
plt.ylabel("Number of Tracks")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('popularity_histogram.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()


# Popularity tier bar chart

Tier_counts = df['popularity_tier'].value_counts().sort_index()
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.bar(Tier_counts.index, Tier_counts.values)
plt.xlabel('Popularity Tier')
plt.ylabel('Number of Tracks')
plt.title('Popularity Tier Distribution')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('popularity_tier_barchart.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()
Skewness: 0.042 Kurtosis: -0.924 Mean: 33.324 Median: 35.0
 
10.1.2 Audio Feature Distribution
In [556]:
# Audio features distribution

all_audio_features = ["danceability", "energy","loudness", "tempo",
                      "acousticness", "instrumentalness", "speechiness", "valence"]

# Skewness of each audio feature

print((round(df[all_audio_features].skew(),3)))

# Histograms for each auido feature (to be shown together for better presentation

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 4, figsize=(18, 8))
axes = axes.flatten()

for i, col in enumerate(all_audio_features):
    axes[i].hist(df[col], bins=50, color='steelblue')
    axes[i].axvline(df[col].mean(), color='red', linestyle='--', label=f"Mean: {df[col].mean():.2f}")
    axes[i].axvline(df[col].median(), color='gold', linestyle='--', label=f"Median: {df[col].median():.2f}")
    axes[i].set_title(col.capitalize())
    axes[i].set_xlabel('Value')
    axes[i].set_ylabel('Count')
    axes[i].legend(fontsize=7)

plt.suptitle('Audio Feature Distributions', fontsize=14)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('eda_audio_features_grid.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()
danceability -0.400 energy -0.599 loudness -2.013 tempo 0.320 acousticness 0.730 instrumentalness 1.738 speechiness 4.645 valence 0.115 dtype: float64
 
10.1.3 Zero Popularity Distribution
In [557]:
# Zero Popularity distribution

# Count tracks with zero popularity

zero_pop = (df['popularity'] == 0).sum()
print("Number of tracks with zero popularity:", zero_pop) # 177 tracks removed

# Pie chart showing distribution between zero and non-zero popularity tracks
plt.figure(figsize=(6, 6))
plt.pie([len(df) - zero_pop, zero_pop],
labels=['Popularity > 0', 'Popularity = 0'],
        autopct='%1.1f%%',
        colors=['steelblue', 'tomato'],
        startangle=90)
plt.title ("Zero Vs Non-Zero Popularity Tracks")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('zero_popularity_piechart.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()


# Top genres with zero popularity

zero_pop_genres = df[df['popularity'] == 0]['track_genre'].value_counts().head(10)
print(zero_pop_genres)

# Barchart visualising the information for better interpretation

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.barh(zero_pop_genres.index[::-1], zero_pop_genres.values[::-1], color='lightblue')
plt.title('Top 10 Genres with Zero Popularity Tracks')
plt.xlabel('Number of Zero-Popularity Tracks')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('eda_zero_genre.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()
Number of tracks with zero popularity: 15843
track_genre jazz 681 iranian 648 soul 610 country 587 latin 585 romance 555 rock 525 alternative 484 dance 463 house 403 Name: count, dtype: int64

print(ex_pop)


# Bar chart showing explicit and non-explicit count

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.bar(['Non-Explicit', 'Explicit'], ex_count.values, color=['steelblue', 'tomato'])
plt.title('Explicit vs Non-Explicit Count')
plt.ylabel('Number of Tracks')
plt.tight_layout()

plt.bar(['Non-Explicit', 'Explicit'], ex_pop.values, color=['steelblue', 'tomato'])
plt.title('Average Popularity by Explicit')
plt.ylabel('Average Popularity Score')
plt.ylim(0, 45)
plt.tight_layout()

10.2 Bivariate Analysis
 
10.2.1 Which Audio Features Covary Most Strongly with Popularity?
 
10.2.2 How Does Each Audio Feature Individually Correlate to Popularity?
In [560]:
# Chosen Top features based on pearson correlation

features = ['danceability', 'energy', 'valence', 'tempo', 'loudness', 'acousticness']

# Generate scatter plot

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(18, 10))
axes = axes.flatten()

for i, col in enumerate(features):
    axes[i].scatter(df[col], df['popularity'], alpha=0.05, color='steelblue', s=5)
    m, b = np.polyfit(df[col], df['popularity'], 1)
    x_line = np.linspace(df[col].min(), df[col].max(), 100)
    axes[i].plot(x_line, m * x_line + b, color='red', linewidth=1.5,
                 label=f'r = {round(df[col].corr(df["popularity"]), 3)}')
    axes[i].set_title(f'{col.capitalize()} vs Popularity')
    axes[i].set_xlabel(col.capitalize())
    axes[i].set_ylabel('Popularity Score')
    axes[i].legend(fontsize=9)

plt.suptitle('Audio Features vs Popularity', fontsize=14)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('scatter_grid.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()

# Get values for slope
for col in features:
    r = round(df[col].corr(df['popularity']), 3)
    m, b = np.polyfit(df[col], df['popularity'], 1)
    print(f"slope = {round(m, 3)}")
slope = 4.421 slope = -0.217 slope = -3.533 slope = 0.01 slope = 0.211 slope = -1.501
In [561]:
# Creating a new variable with just the variables for correlation

corr_cols = ['danceability','energy','loudness','speechiness','acousticness',
             'instrumentalness','liveness','valence','tempo','duration_min','explicit_int']

# Calculate correlation of each feature with popularity
cor = df[corr_cols + ['popularity']].corr()['popularity'].drop('popularity').sort_values()
print(cor.round(3))

# Generate bar chart visualising the correalation

plt.figure(figsize=(9, 6))
colors = ['steelblue' if val < 0 else 'tomato' for val in cor.values]
plt.barh(cor.index, cor.values, color=colors)
plt.axvline(0, color='black', linewidth=0.8)
plt.title('Pearson Correlation of Audio Features with Popularity')
plt.xlabel('Correlation Coefficient (r)')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('eda_correlation.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()
instrumentalness -0.095 speechiness -0.045 valence -0.041 acousticness -0.022 duration_min -0.008 liveness -0.006 energy -0.002 tempo 0.013 danceability 0.034 explicit_int 0.044 loudness 0.047 Name: popularity, dtype: float64
 
10.2.3 Does Genre Significantly Affect Popularity Distribution?
In [562]:
top5_genres = df.groupby('track_genre')['popularity'].mean().sort_values(ascending=False).head(5).index
bot5_genres  = df.groupby('track_genre')['popularity'].mean().sort_values(ascending=True).head(5).index

top5_data = [df[df['track_genre'] == g]['popularity'].values for g in top5_genres]
bot5_data  = [df[df['track_genre'] == g]['popularity'].values for g in bot5_genres]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 6))

axes[0].boxplot(top5_data, tick_labels=top5_genres, patch_artist=True,
                medianprops=dict(color='red', linewidth=2))
axes[0].set_title('Popularity Distribution — Top 5 Genres')
axes[0].set_ylabel('Popularity Score')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Genre')

axes[1].boxplot(bot5_data, tick_labels=bot5_genres, patch_artist=True,
                medianprops=dict(color='red', linewidth=2))
axes[1].set_title('Popularity Distribution — Bottom 5 Genres')
axes[1].set_ylabel('Popularity Score')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Genre')

plt.suptitle('Popularity Distribution by Genre', fontsize=13)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('eda_genre_boxplot.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()
 
10.2.4 Do Tracks of Standard Commerical Length Perform Better?
In [563]:
# Create duration tiers
df['duration_tier'] = pd.cut(df['duration_min'],
    bins=[0, 2.5, 4, 6, 100],
    labels=['Short (<2.5)', 'Standard (2.5-4)', 'Long (4-6)', 'Very Long (>6)'])

# Summary statistics by tier
print("Popularity by duration tier:")
print(df.groupby('duration_tier')['popularity'].agg(['mean', 'median', 'count']).round(2))

# Group popularity values by tier for box plot
short    = df[df['duration_tier'] == 'Short (<2.5)']['popularity'].values
standard = df[df['duration_tier'] == 'Standard (2.5-4)']['popularity'].values
long     = df[df['duration_tier'] == 'Long (4-6)']['popularity'].values
very_long  = df[df['duration_tier'] == 'Very Long (>6)']['popularity'].values

# Generate Box plot
plt.figure(figsize=(9, 6))
plt.boxplot([short, standard, long, very_long],
            tick_labels=['Short\n(<2.5 min)', 'Standard\n(2.5-4 min)',
                         'Long\n(4-6 min)', 'Very Long\n(>6 min)'],
            patch_artist=True,
            medianprops=dict(color='red', linewidth=2))
plt.title('Popularity Distribution by Track Duration Tier')
plt.ylabel('Popularity Score')
plt.xlabel('Duration Tier')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('eda_duration_boxplot.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()
Popularity by duration tier: mean median count duration_tier Short (<2.5) 30.56 29.0 15100 Standard (2.5-4) 34.06 36.0 59386 Long (4-6) 34.34 36.0 31341 Very Long (>6) 28.91 27.0 7722

Pass observed=False to retain current behavior or observed=True to adopt the future default and silence this warning. print(df.groupby('duration_tier')['popularity'].agg(['mean', 'median', 'count']).round(2))
 

10.3 Multivariate Analysis

 
10.3.1 How Do All Features Correlate Simultaneously?
In [564]:
# Generate correlation heat map

corr_cols = ['popularity','danceability','energy','loudness','speechiness',
             'acousticness','instrumentalness','liveness','valence','tempo',
             'duration_min','explicit_int']

corr_matrix = df[corr_cols].corr().round(2)

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 9))
im = plt.imshow(corr_matrix.values, cmap='coolwarm', vmin=-1, vmax=1, aspect='auto')
plt.colorbar(im, fraction=0.046, pad=0.04)
plt.xticks(range(len(corr_cols)), corr_cols, rotation=45, ha='right', fontsize=8)
plt.yticks(range(len(corr_cols)), corr_cols, fontsize=8)
for i in range(len(corr_cols)):
    for j in range(len(corr_cols)):
        val = corr_matrix.values[i, j]
        color = 'white' if abs(val) > 0.6 else 'black'
        plt.text(j, i, f'{val:.2f}', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=6.5, color=color)
plt.title('Correlation Heatmap — All Features', fontsize=13)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('eda_heatmap.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()
 
10.3.2 What Differntiates High Popularity Tracks from Low Popularity Tracks Across Multiple Features?
In [565]:
# Split into high and low popularity groups
high = df[df['popularity'] > 60]
low  = df[df['popularity'] <= 20]

audio_cols = ['danceability','energy','loudness','speechiness',
              'acousticness','instrumentalness','liveness','valence','tempo']

high_means = high[audio_cols].mean().round(3)
low_means  = low[audio_cols].mean().round(3)

print(pd.DataFrame({'High Pop (>60)': high_means, 'Low Pop (<=20)': low_means}))

x = np.arange(len(audio_cols))
w = 0.35

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.bar(x - w/2, high_means.values, w, label='High Popularity (>60)', color='steelblue', alpha=0.85)
plt.bar(x + w/2, low_means.values,  w, label='Low Popularity (<=20)',  color='tomato',    alpha=0.85)
plt.xticks(x, audio_cols, rotation=15, ha='right', fontsize=9)
plt.title('Average Audio Features: High vs Low Popularity Tracks')
plt.ylabel('Average Feature Value')
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('eda_high_vs_low.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()
High Pop (>60) Low Pop (<=20) danceability 0.597 0.573 energy 0.649 0.640 loudness -7.386 -8.425 speechiness 0.077 0.085 acousticness 0.264 0.304 instrumentalness 0.066 0.206 liveness 0.180 0.194 valence 0.486 0.483 tempo 120.834 121.481
 
10.3.3 How do All Audio Feature Change Across Popularity Tiers?
In [566]:
# Define popularity tier order and key features to analyse
tier_order   = ['Very Low', 'Low', 'Medium', 'High', 'Very High']
key_features = ['danceability', 'loudness', 'acousticness', 'instrumentalness']

# Print average feature values per popularity tier
print(df.groupby('popularity_tier')[key_features].mean().round(3).reindex(tier_order))

# Create 2x2 grid of bar charts — one per feature
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(14, 10))
axes = axes.flatten()

for i, col in enumerate(key_features):
    # Calculate mean feature value for each popularity tier
    tier_means = df.groupby('popularity_tier')[col].mean().reindex(tier_order)

    # Plot bar chart
    axes[i].bar(tier_means.index, tier_means.values, color='steelblue', alpha=0.85)
    axes[i].set_title(f'Average {col.capitalize()} by Popularity Tier')
    axes[i].set_xlabel('Popularity Tier')
    axes[i].set_ylabel(f'Average {col.capitalize()}')
    axes[i].tick_params(axis='x', rotation=15)

plt.suptitle('Audio Feature Means Across Popularity Tiers', fontsize=13)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('eda_tier_features.png', dpi=150)
plt.show()
